info.name: Patient Ecosystem POC
info.description:
REST API collection for the Patient Ecosystem POC.

## Getting started

1. Run **Auth / Register** or **Auth / Login** — the test script automatically saves `{{token}}` and `{{patientId}}` as collection variables.
2. All subsequent requests use those variables, so no manual copy-paste is needed.

## Collection variables

| Variable    | Description                                      |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `baseUrl`   | API base URL (default: `http://localhost:3001`)  |
| `token`     | JWT — populated automatically on login/register  |
| `patientId` | Authenticated patient's ID — set on login/register |
| `docId`     | Document ID — set manually for document requests |

## Auth

All endpoints except **Health Check**, **Register**, and **Login** require a valid JWT. The token is sent as `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

Tokens expire after 7 days. Re-run Login to refresh.

request.description: Returns the API status, version, and current timestamp. Use this to confirm the backend is running before making other requests.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Public endpoints for patient registration and login.

Both requests include a test script that saves the returned JWT as `{{token}}` and the patient's ID as `{{patientId}}`. Run either one before using the Patients or Documents folders.

exec: console.log('Token and patientId saved from Register');

request.description:
Creates a new patient account and returns a JWT.

Required fields: `first_name`, `last_name`, `date_of_birth`, `email`, `password` (min 8 characters).

Returns `409` if the email is already registered.

exec: console.log('Token and patientId saved from Login');

request.description:
Authenticates a patient and returns a JWT valid for 7 days.

Returns `401` for an unrecognised email or incorrect password.

item.name: Patients
item.description:
Endpoints for reading and managing the authenticated patient's own record.

All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`. Attempting to access another patient's record returns `403 Access denied`.

request.description: Returns the logged-in patient's record. The backend ignores any query parameters and always returns the record belonging to the token owner.
Returns a single patient record by ID. Returns `403` if `{{patientId}}` does not match the authenticated patient.
Partially updates the patient record. All fields are optional — only supplied fields are changed (COALESCE update). Returns `403` if `{{patientId}}` does not match the authenticated patient.
Permanently deletes the patient record and all associated documents (cascade). Returns `403` if `{{patientId}}` does not match the authenticated patient.

item.name: Documents
item.description:
Endpoints for uploading, listing, downloading, and deleting patient documents.

All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`. Documents are scoped to the authenticated patient — accessing another patient's documents returns `403 Access denied`.

Supported file types: PDF, Word (.doc/.docx), and common image formats. Maximum file size: 10 MB.

request.description: Returns all documents uploaded for the patient, ordered by upload date descending.

Uploads a file for the patient. Use form-data with the following fields:
- `document` (file) — the file to upload
- `document_type` (text) — e.g. `consent_form`, `lab_result`, `insurance`, `general`
- `description` (text, optional) — free-text note about the document
Streams the document file as a download. Set `{{docId}}` to the ID returned by List or Upload. Returns `403` if the document belongs to a different patient.
Deletes the document record from the database and removes the file from disk. Returns `403` if the document belongs to a different patient.
